headline: What Banks Stand to Lose From the Private-Credit Mess
summary: As the start of their earnings season approaches, investors are scrutinizing banks for their exposure to nonbank financial firms, and in particular the private funds that have seen a surge of redemptions.  The KBW Nasdaq Bank Index is down around 6%, versus the S&P 500’s roughly 4.5% decline, so far this year.  There has been a surge in recent years of bank lending to nonbanks, or so-called non-depository financial institutions, or NDFIs.

There has been a surge in recent years of bank lending to nonbanks, or so-called non-depository financial institutions, or NDFIs.

headline: Nike draws downgrades as recovery timeline extends
summary: Investing.com -- Nike shares were downgraded to Neutral by analysts at both JPMorgan and Bank of America on Wednesday after the sportswear giant's latest results pointed to a longer-than-expected road back to growth, with price targets also slashed.

content.title: Nike draws downgrades as recovery timeline extends
content.summary: Investing.com -- Nike shares were downgraded to Neutral by analysts at both JPMorgan and Bank of America on Wednesday after the sportswear giant's latest results pointed to a longer-than-expected road back to growth, with price targets also slashed.

headline: BofA cuts global growth forecast on ‘mild’ stagflation shock
summary: Investing.com -- In a note to clients on Wednesday, Bank of America lowered its global growth outlook for 2026, warning that the economic fallout from the Iran war is delivering what it describes as a “mild stagflation” shock.

headline: Soaring gas prices to push U.S. inflation to nearly 4% in coming months: BofA
summary: Investing.com -- Bank of America is warning that surging energy prices will drive U.S. headline inflation close to 4% in the coming months, with price pressures seen persisting well into next year.

headline: FWONK: Bank of America sees durable growth story, upgrades to buy
summary: Investing.com -- Bank of America upgraded shares of Liberty Media Formula One from Neutral to Buy on Wednesday, citing the company's resilient business model, long-term monetization potential and favorable risk/reward at current valuations.

headline: Prosecutors Link Foiled Bomb Plot at U.S. Bank in Paris to Pro-Iran Group
summary: French counterterrorism prosecutors said on Wednesday that a foiled bomb plot against a Bank of America office in Paris appeared to be linked to a pro-Iranian group known as Harakat al-Ashab al-Yamin al-Islamia.

headline: Oracle Data Center Nears $16 Billion Financing After Twisty Path
summary: (Bloomberg) -- Related Digital is finalizing $16 billion of financing for a giant Oracle Corp. data center, after months of stop-and-start negotiations with investors. The developer has been rounding up cash to build a Michigan campus that would allow Oracle to power applications for OpenAI. The financing follows other massive debt packages that banks assembled for Oracle data centers: a $38 billion debt deal to build facilities in Texas and Wisconsin, and $18 billion for a New Mexico site. The

headline: How The Bruker (BRKR) Story Is Shifting As Growth Hopes Meet Margin And Helium Risks
summary: Bruker’s fair value estimate has been trimmed slightly from US$48.43 to US$47.86 per share, signaling a modest reset in modeled upside. This small move lines up with mixed analyst commentary, where some are encouraged by longer term growth targets while others focus on margin execution, helium related input costs, and peer multiple compression when revisiting price targets. As you read on, you will see how these shifting views fit together and what to watch as the Bruker story continues to...
